title: atalantia roxburghiana var. roxburghiana
intro: Atalantia roxburghiana var. roxburghiana, also known as the Roxburgh's Atalantia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a small, evergreen tree or shrub native to the Himalayas and Southeast Asia, valued for its medicinal properties. The Roxburgh's Atalantia has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal medicine, the plant can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, and has been found to offer benefits such as reducing inflammation, relieving respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system.
